questions.question: NO 2 Br 2 O   P 2 O 5 Which statement about these oxides is correct? A 1, 2 and 3 contain basic oxides only., B 2, 3 and 4 contain basic oxides only., C 1 contains basic oxides only and 4 contains acidic oxides only., D 1 contains acidic oxides only and 4 contains basic oxides only. 9
questions.text: NO 2 Br 2 O   P 2 O 5 Which statement about these oxides is correct? A 1, 2 and 3 contain basic oxides only., B 2, 3 and 4 contain basic oxides only., C 1 contains basic oxides only and 4 contains acidic oxides only., D 1 contains acidic oxides only and 4 contains basic oxides only. 9
options: A 1, 2 and 3 contain basic oxides only.
B 2, 3 and 4 contain basic oxides only.
C 1 contains basic oxides only and 4 contains acidic oxides only.
D 1 contains acidic oxides only and 4 contains basic oxides only. 9

questions.question: A student carries out an experiment to prepare pure magnesium sulfate crystals. The diagram shows the first stage of the preparation. dilute sulfuric acid stirrer magnesium carbonate He adds magnesium carbonate until no more reacts. Which process should he use for the next stage? A crystallisation, B evaporation, C filtration, D neutralisation
questions.text: A student carries out an experiment to prepare pure magnesium sulfate crystals. questions.question: A substance, X, has the following properties. 1   It has a high melting point. 2   It conducts electricity in the solid and liquid states. 3   It is malleable. 4   It has a high density. What is X? A a ceramic, B copper, C graphite, D sodium chloride 11

questions.question: A reactivity series is shown. sodium calcium magnesium carbon zinc iron hydrogen copper Which statement is correct? A All the metals above carbon are extracted by electrolysis., B Iron can only be extracted by electrolysis., C Calcium can be extracted by heating calcium oxide with carbon., D Copper can only be extracted by passing hydrogen over heated copper( II) oxide.
questions.text: A reactivity series is shown. sodium calcium magnesium carbon zinc iron hydrogen copper Which statement is correct? A All the metals above carbon are extracted by electrolysis., B Iron can only be extracted by electrolysis., C Calcium can be extracted by heating calcium oxide with carbon., D Copper can only be extracted by passing hydrogen over heated copper( II) oxide.
options: A All the metals above carbon are extracted by electrolysis.
B Iron can only be extracted by electrolysis.
C Calcium can be extracted by heating calcium oxide with carbon.
D Copper can only be extracted by passing hydrogen over heated copper( II) oxide.

options: A Al B Cu C Fe D Zn

questions.question: Which statement about lime and limestone is correct? A Calcium oxide is formed from limestone in a displacement reaction., B Lime is used to treat alkaline soils., C Limestone is a waste material in the manufacture of iron., D Slaked lime is used in the process of flue gas desulfurisation.
questions.text: Which statement about lime and limestone is correct? A Calcium oxide is formed from limestone in a displacement reaction., B Lime is used to treat alkaline soils., C Limestone is a waste material in the manufacture of iron., D Slaked lime is used in the process of flue gas desulfurisation.
options: A Calcium oxide is formed from limestone in a displacement reaction.
B Lime is used to treat alkaline soils.
C Limestone is a waste material in the manufacture of iron.
D Slaked lime is used in the process of flue gas desulfurisation.

questions.question: Which compound has a chemical name ending in –ol ? A C 2 H 5 OH B C 2 H 6, C CH 3 COOH D C 2 H 4 13

C CH 3 COOH D C 2 H 4 13

questions.question: Petroleum is separated into fractions by fractional distillation. Separation occurs in a fractionating column. Some properties of three of these fractions are shown. fraction boiling point range / C number of carbon atoms in the molecules 1  5 –10 2 320–350 16–24 3 120–210 Which statement is correct? A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2., B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1., C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2., D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature.
questions.text: Petroleum is separated into fractions by fractional distillation. Separation occurs in a fractionating column. Some properties of three of these fractions are shown. fraction boiling point range / C number of carbon atoms in the molecules 1  5 –10 2 320–350 16–24 3 120–210 Which statement is correct? A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2., B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1., C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2., D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature.
options: A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2.
B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1.
C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2.
D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Which statement about alkenes is correct? A Alkenes are saturated hydrocarbons., B Alkenes can be made by cracking other hydrocarbon compounds., C Alkenes change bromine water from colourless to brown., D Alkene molecules contain double bonds between carbon atoms and hydrogen atoms.
questions.text: Which statement about alkenes is correct? A Alkenes are saturated hydrocarbons., B Alkenes can be made by cracking other hydrocarbon compounds., C Alkenes change bromine water from colourless to brown., D Alkene molecules contain double bonds between carbon atoms and hydrogen atoms.
options: B Alkenes can be made by cracking other hydrocarbon compounds.
C Alkenes change bromine water from colourless to brown.
D Alkene molecules contain double bonds between carbon atoms and hydrogen atoms.

questions.question: Which statements about aqueous ethanoic acid are correct? 1   It is an alkane. 2   It reacts with sodium carbonate to form carbon dioxide. 3   It changes the colour of litmus solution from blue to red. 4   It is a hydrocarbon. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 4 C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Which statements about aqueous ethanoic acid are correct? 1   It is an alkane. 2   It reacts with sodium carbonate to form carbon dioxide. 3   It changes the colour of litmus solution from blue to red. 4   It is a hydrocarbon. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 4 C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  1 and 4 C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4
